meanings.meaning: （通常指小商贩出售的）货物，商品
meanings.usage: 常用复数形式wares，多指手工制作或沿街售卖的小商品
指定期航行的大型客运轮船
指船只放下锚固定在水面
指船只停泊时使用的金属器具
不可数名词，泛指银制的餐具、饰品等
常用搭配：tempt sb. to do sth. 诱惑某人做某事；be tempted to do sth. 很想做某事
常用搭配：bargain with sb. about/over sth. 和某人就某物议价
指价格低于实际价值的商品，或达成的买卖协议

meanings.meaning: （从船、飞机、火车上）登陆，下车，下机
meanings.usage: 正式用词，指离开交通工具，尤其指船只
常用搭配：be assailed by sb./sth. 被某人/某事纠缠/困扰
正式用词，搭配：conceal sth. from sb. 向某人隐瞒某事
表示“弹珠”时为可数名词，表示“大理石”时为不可数名词
常用被动语态：be inscribed on/in sth. 被刻在某物上
常用搭配：do sb. a favour 帮某人一个忙；as a special favour 作为特别优惠
指说话时用手势辅助表达情绪或意思

meanings.meaning: 骇人的，离谱的，无法接受的
meanings.usage: 可用来形容价格、行为等离谱到令人难以接受
不规则动词，过去式和过去分词均为thrust，搭配：thrust sth. into sb.'s hands 把某物塞到某人手里

phrases.phrase: go to great lengths to do sth.
phrases.usage: 表示“竭尽全力做某事，不遗余力做某事”，含为达成目的不惜花费时间精力的含义

examples.en: The man went to great lengths to prove that the diamonds were real.
examples.zh: 那个男子竭尽全力证明这些钻石是真的。

examples.en: Many companies go to great lengths to protect their trade secrets.
examples.zh: 许多公司不遗余力地保护自己的商业机密。

phrases.usage: 表示“摆脱，除去，处理掉”，可接人或物作宾语

examples.en: It took me over half an hour to get rid of him.
examples.zh: 我花了半个多小时才摆脱掉他。

examples.en: I need to get rid of these old clothes I never wear.
examples.zh: 我需要把这些我从来不穿的旧衣服处理掉。

phrases.usage: 表示“作为特别优惠/优待”，常用于商家说服顾客购买商品的语境

examples.en: As a special favour, he would let me have it for ￡30.
examples.zh: 作为特别优惠，他愿意收我30英镑就把它卖给我。

examples.en: The shop owner gave me the discount as a special favour for regular customers.
examples.zh: 作为给老顾客的特别优待，店主给了我这个折扣。

phrases.usage: 表示“一…就…”，引导时间状语从句，no sooner位于句首时句子要部分倒装

examples.en: I had no sooner got off the ship than I was assailed by a man who wanted to sell me a diamond ring.
examples.zh: 我一下船就被一个想要卖给我钻石戒指的男子缠住了。

examples.en: No sooner had we sat down than the film started.
examples.zh: 我们刚一坐下电影就开始了。

phrases.phrase: have no intention of doing sth.
phrases.usage: 表示“没有做某事的打算，无意做某事”，强调主观上完全不想做

examples.en: I had no intention of buying one, but I could not conceal the fact that I was impressed by the size of the diamonds.
examples.zh: 我根本不想买（钻戒），但我无法掩饰的是，那些钻石的大小确实给我留下了深刻的印象。

examples.en: She has no intention of giving up her career after getting married.
examples.zh: 她完全没有打算在结婚后放弃自己的事业。

phrases.usage: 表示“对…印象深刻，被…打动”，主语是人，宾语是令人印象深刻的事物

examples.en: I was impressed by the efficiency of the local government.
examples.zh: 当地政府的办事效率给我留下了深刻的印象。

phrases.phrase: throw up one's arms in despair
phrases.usage: 表示“绝望地举起双手，无可奈何”，用来表达失望、无力的肢体动作和情绪

examples.en: Though he kept throwing up his arms in despair, he readily accepted the ￡5 I gave him.
examples.zh: 尽管他一直绝望地挥舞着手臂，但还是欣然接受了我付给他的5英镑。

examples.en: When he found all his efforts had been in vain, he threw up his arms in despair.
examples.zh: 当他发现自己所有的努力都白费了时，他绝望地举起了双手。

phrases.usage: 表示“对…感到满意/高兴”，后接名词或动名词

examples.en: I felt especially pleased with my wonderful bargain.
examples.zh: 我为自己这笔绝妙的交易感到格外得意。

examples.en: The manager was very pleased with the performance of the new employee.
examples.zh: 经理对新员工的表现非常满意。

grammar.title: 过去完成时（Past Perfect Tense）
grammar.definition: 表示过去某个动作或时间之前已经完成的动作，即“过去的过去”，强调动作发生的先后顺序
grammar.structure: 主语 + had + 动词过去分词
grammar.usage: 常用于描述在过去某个明确动作之前已经发生的事，常和before, until, no sooner...than, hardly...when等结构搭配使用

examples.en: Before she had anchored, the men from the boats had climbed on board.
examples.zh: 大船还没下锚，小船上的人就已经爬上了船。

examples.en: I decided not to buy anything until I had disembarked.
examples.zh: 我决定在下船之前什么都不买。

examples.en: He had finished his homework before his parents came back home.
examples.zh: 在父母回家之前他就已经做完作业了。

grammar.title: 让步状语从句（Adverbial Clause of Concession）
grammar.definition: 用来引导与主句内容相反或相对的信息，表达“虽然，尽管，即使”等让步含义，主句和从句之间存在转折关系
grammar.structure: 从属连词（though/although/no matter how等）+ 从句，主句
grammar.usage: though和although表示“虽然”，不与but连用；no matter + 疑问词（how/what/when等）表示“无论/不管…”，引导让步状语从句

examples.en: Though he kept throwing up his arms in despair, he readily accepted the ￡5 I gave him.
examples.zh: 尽管他一直绝望地挥舞着手臂，但还是欣然接受了我付给他的5英镑。

examples.en: No matter how hard I tried, it was impossible to fill this beautiful pen with ink.
examples.zh: 不管我怎么努力，都没法把墨水灌进这支漂亮的钢笔里。

examples.en: Although it was raining heavily, he still went out to meet his friend.
examples.zh: 尽管雨下得很大，他还是出门去见朋友了。

grammar.title: 并列结构中的省略
grammar.definition: 在并列句中，如果前后分句的主语和谓语动词相同，为了避免重复，可省略后一分句中相同的部分
grammar.structure: 分句1 + 连词 + （省略相同成分的）分句2
grammar.usage: 常见于and, but, or等连词连接的并列句中，省略后句子结构更简洁，语义不受影响

examples.en: The decks were soon covered with colourful rugs from Persia, silks from India, copper coffee pots, and beautiful handmade silverware.
examples.zh: 甲板上很快就摆满了来自波斯的彩色地毯、印度的丝绸、铜制咖啡壶，还有精美的手工银器。

examples.en: It was difficult not to be tempted.
examples.zh: 很难不动心。

examples.en: My friend likes reading novels, and I (like) reading poems.
examples.zh: 我的朋友喜欢读小说，我喜欢读诗歌。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It takes/took sb. + 时间 + to do sth.

original.en: It took me over half an hour to get rid of him.
original.zh: 我花了半个多小时才摆脱掉他。

imitations.en: It took me three hours to finish this difficult math problem.
imitations.zh: 我花了三个小时才解出这道很难的数学题。

imitations.en: It took the workers two years to build this bridge.
imitations.zh: 工人们花了两年时间才建成这座桥。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + had no sooner done sth. than + 一般过去时从句

original.en: I had no sooner got off the ship than I was assailed by a man who wanted to sell me a diamond ring.
original.zh: 我一下船就被一个想要卖给我钻石戒指的男子缠住了。

imitations.en: We had no sooner arrived at the station than the train left.
imitations.zh: 我们刚到车站，火车就开了。

imitations.en: He had no sooner finished his speech than the audience started clapping.
imitations.zh: 他刚结束演讲，观众就开始鼓掌了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + 动词 + when, a moment later + 另一动作

original.en: Shrugging my shoulders I began to walk away when, a moment later, he ran after me and thrust the pen into my hands.
original.zh: 我耸了耸肩转身就走，这时，他追了上来，把钢笔塞到了我手里。

imitations.en: I was about to close the door when, a moment later, my neighbour knocked and asked for a cup of sugar.
imitations.zh: 我正要关门，这时我的邻居敲门来要一杯糖。

imitations.en: We were just leaving the café when, a moment later, it started to pour with rain.
imitations.zh: 我们正要离开咖啡馆，这时突然下起了倾盆大雨。

sentencePatterns.pattern: No matter how + 形容词/副词 + 主语 + 谓语, 主句

original.en: No matter how hard I tried, it was impossible to fill this beautiful pen with ink and to this day it has never written a single word!
original.zh: 不管我怎么努力，都没法把墨水灌进这支漂亮的钢笔里，直到今天它也没写出过一个字！

imitations.en: No matter how difficult the task is, I will try my best to complete it.
imitations.zh: 不管这项任务有多难，我都会尽全力完成它。

imitations.en: No matter how many times he failed, he never gave up his dream.
imitations.zh: 不管他失败了多少次，他从来没有放弃自己的梦想。
